title: Non-constant static fields should not be visible
description: Non-constant static fields should not be visibleAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoUnlike instance fields, which can only be accessed by code having a hold on the instance,staticfields can be accessed by any code having visibility of the field and its type.public class Math { public static double Pi = 3.14; // Noncompliant } // Somewhere else, where Math and Math.Pi are visible var pi = Math.Pi; // Reading the value Math.Pi = 3.1416; // Mutating the valueAnother typical scenario of the use of a non-private mutablestaticfield is the following:public class Shape { public static Shape Empty = new EmptyShape(); // Noncompliant private class EmptyShape : Shape { } }Non-privatestaticfields that are neitherconstnorreadonly, like
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.triggering_conditions: CompilationUnit nodes containing non-private static field declarations that are not const or readonly
never_trigger_on: readonly fields (public static readonly string Name)
logic.reasoning: Detects non-private static fields that lack const or readonly modifiers, making them mutable from external code. Uses CompilationUnit with a precise pattern that includes negative lookaheads for const/readonly and requires assignment (=) to distinguish from properties. No exclude patterns needed since the pattern is specific enough.

should_trigger: protected static Shape Empty = new Shape();
should_not_trigger: public static readonly Shape Empty = new Shape();
public static int Counter { get; set; }
